howItWorks.intro: This meat resting time calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Rest Time
rules.explanation: Larger cuts need more resting time for juices to redistribute through the full volume.

rules.name: Pull Temperature
rules.formula: Pull Temp = Target Temp - Carryover Rise
rules.explanation: Remove meat from heat before it reaches target, accounting for continued internal temperature rise.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: Why does meat need to rest?
faq.a: During cooking, heat drives moisture toward the center. Resting allows juices to redistribute evenly throughout the meat. Cutting too soon causes juices to pour out, leaving the meat drier. A rested steak can retain up to 50% more juices.

The internal temperature of meat continues to rise 5-15 degrees F after removal from heat. Larger cuts carry over more. Pull your meat from the heat when it is that many degrees below your target temperature.
